projectDescription: An AI-native adversarial peer validation protocol that forces structured verdicts, executable steps, and decision state you can paste back into subsequent runs.
keyFeatures: Forced verdict output — every run produces DO NOW, DO LATER, or DISCARD with no vague 'it depends' answers
Stateful ping-pong loop — paste a previous output back to continue delta-only from where you left off
AI-to-AI adversarial validation — enables peer LLMs to challenge and patch each other's decisions
Scalable surface — stays minimal for low-impact decisions and expands to full debug structure for contested or high-risk items
Gates and probes — systematic reality probing, falsification debt tracking, and dominant gate routing
Executable steps with grammar — steps are observable, timebox-bounded, and have explicit fail conditions
AskUser contract switch — controls whether the kernel is allowed to request external operator evidence
SNAPSHOT-AS-INPUT — paste an entire prior output as input for no-look resumption with zero manual editing
publicApiSurface: <<<B keep_open=NO expire_days=30 exec_capability=NO_RUNTIME close_policy=SIM_OK ssot_scale=MIN resource_pool=>>> (batch header)
<<<I id=E1 title="..." keep_open=NO expire_days=30 depends_on= uses= reversibility=>>> (item wrapper)
REPORT / REPORT LITE (read-only derived report commands)
[QUEUE] (machine scan surface with id | verdict | impact | urgency | gate | step1)
AskUser = ON | OFF (contract switch for operator evidence requests)
